behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is real watcher recursion over OS filesystem events (MOCK-MISS + REAL-INFRA + MOCK-HIDE). A mocked watcher can emit synthetic subdirectory events and pass even if the real watcher backend never emits them. Rejected lower level: behavior text explicitly requires handling files placed in subdirectories of the watched root, which depends on real watch semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Run the daemon against a real temp directory tree, create a nested subdirectory after startup, write a file there, and assert exactly one transformed output appears in the output directory without restarting the daemon.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is local decision logic keyed by path + time window (MOCK-MISS says single-function/stateful object logic). No real OS/network/subprocess needed (REAL-INFRA no). No model output (LLM-DEP no). Rejected higher levels: behavior text is specifically dedup logic within a configurable window, which can be exercised deterministically with a fake clock.
behaviors.test_description: Test the dedup component directly: submit request A for path P at t0, submit request B for path P at t0+delta<window, assert B is discarded; then at t0+delta>window assert request is accepted.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly targets OS-emitted duplicate events, so real filesystem event behavior is the failure boundary (REAL-INFRA yes). In-process event fakes can miss timing/coalescing patterns and hide real duplication bugs (MOCK-HIDE yes). Rejected Integration: text says operating system emits multiple filesystem events.
behaviors.test_description: With daemon running on a real watched directory, perform a single write pattern known to trigger multiple events (e.g., write+rename/temp-file replace) and assert only one pipeline execution/output occurs for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: Directory deletion/recreation changes real filesystem state/inodes and watcher subscriptions at OS level (REAL-INFRA yes). Mocked watchers often auto-heal unrealistically and hide reattach failures (MOCK-HIDE yes). Rejected Integration: behavior text requires remove/recreate while running, which is an OS-level watch lifecycle condition.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon watching a real directory, delete it, recreate it at the same path, then add a new file and verify it is processed without restarting the daemon process.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is module handoff/order logic between intake queue and sorter (MOCK-MISS at module boundary), but it does not require real OS event semantics if inputs include creation timestamps directly. INTEGRATION-BRAKE checks: no required real subprocess/network; no AI dependence; no multi-agent chain. Rejected System: behavior text focuses on ordering by creation timestamps, not OS watcher event correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Wire intake, ordering, and pipeline modules in-process. Inject multiple arrival requests with controlled creation timestamps (including close ties), run scheduler, and assert processing order matches ascending creation timestamp contract.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: This is a real filesystem timing/partial-write condition tied to OS events and write completion detection (REAL-INFRA yes). A mock writer or fake event stream can hide races around file stability checks (MOCK-HIDE yes). Rejected Integration: behavior text explicitly depends on arrival event firing before write completion.
behaviors.test_description: Using a real watched directory, begin writing a large file in chunks with delays after first chunk triggers arrival; assert daemon does not process during active writes and processes once after final flush/close.
